notes.content: The Computer Engineering Bachelor of Science program is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET under the commission's General Criteria and Program Criteria for Electrical, Computer, Communications, Telecommunication(s), and Similarly Named Engineering Programs. The Electrical & Computer Engineering program educational objectives have been developed to address the needs of our constituencies. The objectives of the Electrical & Computer Engineering programs are as follow: Graduates will create value for their employers, demonstrating knowledge and initiative and making beneficial contributions beyond the workplace. This can also result in patents, awards, publications and presentations. Graduates will grow their capabilities through advanced education and professional development. Graduates will provide leadership and be proactive in their profession and/or communities.
notes.contentHtml:
The Computer Engineering Bachelor of Science program is accredited by the Engineering Accreditation Commission of ABET under the commission's General Criteria and Program Criteria for Electrical, Computer, Communications, Telecommunication(s), and Similarly Named Engineering Programs.


The Electrical & Computer Engineering program educational objectives have been developed to address the needs of our constituencies. The objectives of the Electrical & Computer Engineering programs are as follow:


  • Graduates will create value for their employers, demonstrating knowledge and initiative and making beneficial contributions beyond the workplace. This can also result in patents, awards, publications and presentations.


  • Graduates will grow their capabilities through advanced education and professional development.


  • Graduates will provide leadership and be proactive in their profession and/or communities.

notes.content: This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students need to meet a minimum 3.10 GPA cumulative and a 3.20 GPA in major preparation coursework. All majors in the College of Engineering are selective and have additional transfer requirements. Important Information: Complete the courses below: ● Communication (CMN) 001 or Engineering (ENG) 003 ● Engineering (ENG) 017 ● Engineering Computer Science (ECS) 020 and 036A/B/C ● Mathematics (MAT) 021A/B/C/D ● Mathematics (MAT) 022A/B ● Physics (PHY) 009A/B/C ● English (ENL) 003 OR University Writing Program (UWP) 001 Visit Department agreements as needed Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions ( Find my advisor ) Note: Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination: AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog . Quick reference charts for AP and IB .
notes.contentHtml:
This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students need to meet a minimum 3.10 GPA cumulative and a 3.20 GPA in major preparation coursework.


All majors in the College of Engineering are selective and have additional transfer requirements.


Important Information:


Complete the courses below:

●    Communication (CMN) 001 or Engineering (ENG) 003

●    Engineering (ENG) 017

●    Engineering Computer Science (ECS) 020 and 036A/B/C

●    Mathematics (MAT) 021A/B/C/D

●    Mathematics (MAT) 022A/B

●    Physics (PHY) 009A/B/C

●    English (ENL) 003 OR University Writing Program (UWP) 001


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

notes.content: At the time of TAG submission, the College of Engineering requires a minimum 3.50 cumulative GPA and at least a 3.50 GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
notes.contentHtml: At the time of TAG submission, the College of Engineering requires a minimum 3.50 cumulative GPA and at least a 3.50 GPA across the entire group of courses with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
